html:
Philip Johan von Strahlenberg, also known as Stralenberg, and before his ennoblement in 1707, Tabbert, was born in October 1676 in Stralsund, Swedish Pomerania. He died on September 2, 1747, at Fröllinge in the parish of Getinge, Halland County. He was a Swedish officer and cartographer of Pomeranian origin.

== Biography == Von Strahlenberg entered military service in 1694. In 1701, he became a regimental quartermaster, and in 1703, he was promoted to captain in the Södermanland Regiment. He participated in battles during the siege of Thorn (1703) and in the battles of Fraustadt (1706), Holowczyn (1708), and Poltava (1709).

After the Battle of Poltava, while attempting to find and rescue his brother, he was captured and taken as a prisoner to Siberia, where he remained for several years. During this time, he undertook numerous journeys throughout the region and, together with Johan Anton von Matérn, created a map of Siberia that came into the hands of Peter the Great, who expressed great admiration for it. The locations on the map were determined by Strahlenberg based partly on measurements of roads made by himself and his companions, and partly through astronomical observations during solar eclipses. A particularly important geographical determination he made was of the northeasternmost point of Asia. He received information about that region from two Swedes, a corporal and a lieutenant, who had been sent there by Siberia's governor, Matvey Gagarin. The former built a ship with which he crossed what is now known as the Bering Strait. The return journey took six days, but it is uncertain whether he reached the coast of America or landed on one of the Aleutian Islands; however, he was able to prove that Asia and America were not connected, a belief that was otherwise thought to have been definitively established by Vitus Bering.

Despite the Tsar himself offering Strahlenberg a position leading a surveying office in Russian service, he returned to Sweden after the peace treaty (1721), weakened and ill. He received the rank of lieutenant colonel but retained his previous captain's pay grade. In 1740, he was appointed commandant of Karlshamn fortress, and in 1746, he became a major in the Södermanland regiment.

After returning to Stockholm in 1730, he wrote several books about Russian geography, particularly focusing on the Asian parts of Russia (Das Nord und Östliche Theil von Europa und Asia, 1730). These works contain detailed information, including about the languages spoken in the eastern regions of the Russian Empire, and Strahlenberg is considered a pioneer in the study of these languages. After returning to Sweden, he settled on his brother's estate, Fröllinge, in Getinge, Halland, and he is buried in the Getinge churchyard.

== Posthumous Recognition == In 1932, Strahlenbergsgatan in the Hammarbyhöjden district of Stockholm was named after him. Stralenberg is a main character in the Russian novel "Tobol" by Aleksej Ivanov (2017) and its film adaptation (2018). Asteroid 15766 Strahlenberg

== Works == Philip Johan von Strahlenberg. *Vorbericht eines zum Druck verfertigten Werckes von der Grossen Tartarey und dem Königreiche Siberien. mit einem Anhang von Groß-Russland.* Schneider, Stockholm 1726. Philip Johan von Strahlenberg. *Das Nord- und Östliche Theil von Europa und Asia, in so weit solches das gantze Russische Reich mit Sibirien und der grossen Tatarey in sich begriffet.* In Verlegung des Autoris, Stockholm 1730.

== See also == Johan Gustaf Renat

== Sources == This article is wholly or partially based on material from Nordisk familjebok, Stralenberg, Filip Johan, 1904–1926. Strindberg, August. "Philipp Johann von Strahlenberg, his map and description of Asia." *Kulturhistoriska studier.* Stockholm: Albert Bonniers förlag, 1912.

== Further Reading == Berg, Lasse (1992). “One of our first explorers.” *Populär historia* (Lund: Populär historia, 1991–) 1992:2, pp. 44–45. ISSN 1102-0822. ISSN 1102-0822 ISSN 1102-0822. https://popularhistoria.se/vetenskap/upptackare/stralenberg-svensk-upptackare-utforskade-sibirien. Libris 2362767

tldr: Philip Johan von Strahlenberg was a Swedish officer and cartographer who became a prisoner in Siberia during a war in the 18th century. While there, he created important maps of the region and helped to prove that Asia and America were not connected.
lattlast:
Philip Johan von Strahlenberg was a Swedish officer who served in the military during the 18th century. After a battle in Russia in 1709, he was captured and sent to Siberia, a region far east of Russia. Despite being a prisoner, he traveled extensively throughout the area and created detailed maps of the landscape.

During his time in Siberia, he worked on measuring roads and observing the stars to determine the locations of various places. He produced a map that was highly valued by the Russian Tsar. He also successfully demonstrated that the northeastern tip of Asia was not connected to America, which was an important contribution to contemporary knowledge of the world.

After the war ended in 1721, he returned to Sweden. He later became known for writing books about the geography of Eastern Europe and the languages spoken there. Today, a street in Stockholm is named after him, and his name is also used in modern books and films.
